DOB: 02/16/1958 MEDICAL RECORD #: 875003035 ACCOUNT NUMBER: 501361583 DATE OF ADMISSION: 02/16/2026 DATE OF SERVICE: 02/17/2026 REFERRING PHYSICIAN MD Amir Shuja MD ADMITTING PHYSICIAN MD Munawar A. Qurashi MD The patient was admitted to my service yesterday here at the Encompass Health Desert Canyon Inpatient Rehab Hospital. PRINCIPAL DIAGNOSES: 1. Myopathy in the setting of severe sepsis, septic shock with right perirectal and perianal abscess, with necrotizing fasciitis. 2. Status post debridement, incision and drainage, and wound VAC placement. 3. Erythema myelosis. 4. Acute blood loss anemia. 5. Dyslipidemia. 6. COPD. 7. Protein calorie malnutrition. 8. Hypertension. HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS: A 67-year-old female, she lives with the family, was ambulatory prior, was admitted to the hospital with rectal pain for the past several months with a bowel movement and recently with the bloody stools. She was admitted to this hospital and workup reveals she has necrotizing fasciitis of right perirectal and perianal area with the abscess size of about 5.8 cm and right medial and gluteal/ischiorectal fossa abscess 4.2 X 2.2 cm. She was followed by the surgeon, taken over to the OR for incision and drainage and debridement for necrotizing wound infection. She was placed on antibiotics, followed by the ID. Wound care was continued. Wound VAC was also placed in. Report Request ID: 639252784 Page 1 of 3 Print Date/Time: 2/24/2026 11:39 CS Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Desert Canyon Patient: LAMBERT, BARBARA J MRN: 875003035 Admit Date: 2/16/2026 FIN#: 501361583 Discharge Date: DOB/Age/Gender: 2/16/1958 68 years Female Attending Phys: Qurashi Munawar A History and Physical The patient had pericardial effusion. CT chest was done and no further intervention needed. She had echo, which shows ejection fraction with 55-60%. The patient's surgeon, Dr. Chan continued wound care and extensive rehabilitation program as she is significantly weak and she has been on oxygen. PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: As mentioned above. PAST SURGICAL HISTORY: As above. SOCIAL HISTORY: She used to smoke 0.5 pack per day but currently she smokes 1 pack cigarettes a day. No history of any alcohol use. FAMILY HISTORY: Noncontributory. ALLERGIES: No known drug allergies. CURRENT MEDICATIONS: She is on, 1. Augmentin 875 mg 1 tablet p.o. q.12 hours. 2. Norvasc 5 mg p.o. at bedtime. 3. Lasix 40 mg IV daily. 4. Potassium chloride 20 mEq p.o. daily. 5. Hypochlorite topical apply daily. 6. For pain she is taking Tylenol p.r.n. and oxycodone p.r.n. 10 mg every 4 hours. REVIEW OF SYSTEMS: She is a pleasant female. She is lying in the bed. She is on oxygen. She seems a little anxious. She talks about the wound on the back. I explained to her that we have a wound doctor who will be following the patient. She denies any headache, any dizziness, chest pain, shortness of breath at rest, but with exertion she gets short of breath. She is on oxygen. She denies any back pain. She denies any numbness, tingling sensation. She denies any bladder symptoms. 10 systems are reviewed. PHYSICAL EXAMINATION: GENERAL: Alert, awake, and oriented. VITAL SIGNS: Current vital signs are blood pressure 124/66, pulse 85, respirations 18, temperature 98.3. HEENT EXAMINATION: Head is atraumatic. Conjunctivae anicteric. NECK: Supple. CHEST: Chest sounds decreased respiratory effort. HEART: S1 and S2 audible. ABDOMEN: Soft. Bowel sounds positive. LOWER EXTREMITIES: There are no signs of clubbing or edema noted. MUSCULOSKELETAL: Upper extremity strength 4+/5. Lower extremities is about 4/5. Sensation seem grossly intact. LABS: Today as follows: CBC shows WBC 6.9, hemoglobin 8.7, hematocrit 28.5, and the Report Request ID: 639252784 Page 2 of 3 Print Date/Time: 2/24/2026 11:39 CS⁻ Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Desert Canyon Patient: LAMBERT, BARBARA J MRN: 875003035 Admit Date: 2/16/2026 FIN#: 501361583 Discharge Date: DOB/Age/Gender: 2/16/1958 68 years Female Attending Phys: Qurashi Munawar A History and Physical platelet count is 241. Comprehensive metabolic panel normals are ALT is 33, AST is 9, total protein 5.8. DISCUSSION AND PLAN OF CARE: Based on post-admission evaluation, the patient is a good candidate for inpatient rehab hospitalization. The patient had perirectal abscess, being treated for sepsis, septic shock, acute renal failure secondary to acute tubular necrosis, COPD, bloody stools, pericardial effusion with protein-calorie malnutrition with impaired balance gait, transfer, ADLs. REHAB DIAGNOSIS: Myopathy related to the above, who is currently being admitted to the inpatient rehab facility here yesterday. In addition the following areas: 1. Current functional status of the patient compared to her pre-admission screening evaluation is about min assist with bed mobility, transfers compared to pre-admission screening evaluation is not much different. 2. Medical necessity for inpatient rehab rather than at subacute level of care, continue the medical management and continue transfer medications, continue the wound care and internist will also be following the patient, continue p.o. antibiotics. All this care better served in acute rehab setting rather than patient at subacute level of care. 3. Patient will undergo comprehensive rehab training that includes services of PT, OT working with her at least 3 hours a day 5 days a week and 24-hour nursing care will be provided to her. Physiatrist will supervise her rehab training program and the caseworker will be helping with discharge planning. PROGNOSIS: Fair. LENGTH OF STAY IN REHAB: About 2-3 weeks. DISPOSITION: Home with the family.
